agent_output: {"executive_summary":"Integration architecture for a logistics platform connecting three external systems (SAP ERP via SFTP/XML, DHL Carrier API via REST/webhooks, Analytics Platform via Kafka). The design uses an Anti-Corruption Layer per external system, a central domain event bus for internal decoupling, and distinct integration adapters that translate between the platform's ubiquitous language and each external system's protocol and data model. The architecture ensures that no external system's model leaks into the core domain, each integration point can evolve independently, and the 500ms webhook SLA is met through async-first processing.","architecture":{"components":[{"name":"Order Domain Service","responsibility":"Owns the lifecycle of orders: placement, confirmation, and all associated business rules and invariants.","boundaries":"Publishes OrderPlaced, OrderConfirmed domain events. Never directly communicates with external systems. Only speaks the internal ubiquitous language."},{"name":"Shipment Domain Service","responsibility":"Owns shipment lifecycle: creation, delivery attempts, completion, failure. Enforces shipment invariants (e.g., cannot complete a failed shipment).","boundaries":"Publishes ShipmentCreated, DeliveryAttempted, DeliveryCompleted, DeliveryFailed. Consumes OrderConfirmed to trigger shipment creation workflows."},{"name":"Internal Event Bus","responsibility":"Mediates all domain events between internal services. Provides guaranteed at-least-once delivery, ordering per aggregate, and replay capability.","boundaries":"Internal only. No external system connects directly. Implemented as a durable message broker (e.g., internal Kafka topics or similar). All domain events flow through here before reaching any integration adapter."},{"name":"SAP ERP Integration Adapter (ACL)","responsibility":"Anti-Corruption Layer for System A. Subscribes to OrderConfirmed events, transforms them into SAP-specific XML format, batches them, and delivers via SFTP on the 15-minute schedule. Also handles SFTP connection management, retry, and file naming conventions.","boundaries":"Owns the SAP XML schema mapping. Encapsulates SFTP protocol details. No SAP concepts leak upstream. Maintains its own outbox table for reliable batching."},{"name":"DHL Carrier Integration Adapter (ACL)","responsibility":"Anti-Corruption Layer for System B. Two sub-components: (1) Outbound — subscribes to ShipmentCreated events and calls DHL REST API to book shipments. (2) Inbound — exposes a webhook endpoint that receives DHL status callbacks, validates/authenticates them, acknowledges within 500ms, and translates DHL status models into internal domain events (DeliveryAttempted, DeliveryCompleted, DeliveryFailed).","boundaries":"Owns the DHL data model translation. Webhook handler is deliberately thin — acknowledges immediately and enqueues for async domain processing. No DHL-specific types escape this adapter."},{"name":"Analytics Event Stream Adapter","responsibility":"Subscribes to ALL domain events on the internal event bus and projects them onto the external Kafka topic(s) in the Analytics Platform's expected schema. Handles schema evolution and backward compatibility.","boundaries":"Read-only consumer of internal events. Owns the analytics event schema (Published Language). Does not feed data back into the domain."},{"name":"Webhook Gateway","responsibility":"Thin HTTP layer that receives inbound webhooks from DHL (and potentially other carriers in the future). Validates signatures, performs rate limiting, returns 200 ACK within the 500ms SLA, and enqueues the raw payload for async processing by the DHL Integration Adapter.","boundaries":"Protocol-level only. No business logic. Stateless. Horizontally scalable."},{"name":"SFTP Scheduler","responsibility":"Cron-triggered component that flushes the SAP outbox every 15 minutes. Assembles queued OrderConfirmed XML documents into a single batch file, uploads via SFTP, and marks records as sent.","boundaries":"Operates on the SAP adapter's outbox. Handles SFTP connection pooling, retries with exponential backoff, and duplicate suppression via idempotency keys in filenames."}],"interfaces":[{"from":"Order Domain Service","to":"Internal Event Bus","contract":"Publishes OrderPlaced{orderId, timestamp, lineItems, customer} and OrderConfirmed{orderId, timestamp, confirmedBy}","coupling_type":"event (async, fire-and-forget)"},{"from":"Shipment Domain Service","to":"Internal Event Bus","contract":"Publishes ShipmentCreated{shipmentId, orderId, carrier, trackingNumber}, DeliveryAttempted{shipmentId, attemptNumber, timestamp}, DeliveryCompleted{shipmentId, timestamp, signature}, DeliveryFailed{shipmentId, reason, timestamp}","coupling_type":"event (async, fire-and-forget)"},{"from":"Internal Event Bus","to":"SAP ERP Integration Adapter","contract":"Delivers OrderConfirmed events. Adapter transforms to SAP XML and writes to outbox.","coupling_type":"event subscription (async, at-least-once)"},{"from":"SAP ERP Integration Adapter","to":"SAP ERP (System A)","contract":"SFTP upload of XML batch file conforming to SAP IDOC schema. Filename contains timestamp and sequence number for idempotency.","coupling_type":"file transfer (batch, scheduled)"},{"from":"Internal Event Bus","to":"DHL Carrier Integration Adapter","contract":"Delivers ShipmentCreated events. Adapter calls DHL booking API.","coupling_type":"event subscription (async, at-least-once)"},{"from":"DHL Carrier Integration Adapter","to":"DHL API (System B)","contract":"REST POST to DHL shipment booking endpoint. Expects 201 with tracking number. Retries with exponential backoff on 5xx.","coupling_type":"synchronous REST (outbound call)"},{"from":"DHL API (System B)","to":"Webhook Gateway","contract":"HTTP POST callback with DHL shipment status payload. Gateway returns 200 within 500ms.","coupling_type":"webhook (inbound, async processing after ACK)"},{"from":"Webhook Gateway","to":"DHL Carrier Integration Adapter","contract":"Enqueues raw DHL webhook payload onto internal processing queue for translation into domain events.","coupling_type":"queue (async, at-least-once)"},{"from":"DHL Carrier Integration Adapter","to":"Shipment Domain Service","contract":"Calls command methods (RecordDeliveryAttempt, CompleteDelivery, FailDelivery) after translating DHL status.","coupling_type":"synchronous command invocation"},{"from":"Internal Event Bus","to":"Analytics Event Stream Adapter","contract":"All six domain event types. Adapter transforms to analytics schema and produces to Kafka.","coupling_type":"event subscription (async, at-least-once)"},{"from":"Analytics Event Stream Adapter","to":"Analytics Platform (System C)","contract":"Kafka producer writing to designated topic(s) in Avro/JSON schema with schema registry for evolution.","coupling_type":"event stream (async, real-time)"}],"data_flow":"1. OrderPlaced → Internal Event Bus → Analytics Adapter → Kafka (System C). 2. OrderConfirmed → Internal Event Bus → (a) SAP Adapter → outbox → SFTP Scheduler → XML batch → SAP (System A), (b) Shipment Domain Service triggers ShipmentCreated, (c) Analytics Adapter → Kafka. 3. ShipmentCreated → Internal Event Bus → (a) DHL Adapter → REST call → DHL API (System B), (b) Analytics Adapter → Kafka. 4. DHL webhook → Webhook Gateway → ACK 200 → queue → DHL Adapter → translates to DeliveryAttempted/Completed/Failed → Shipment Domain Service → domain event → Internal Event Bus → Analytics Adapter → Kafka.","patterns_used":[{"pattern":"Anti-Corruption Layer (per external system)","rationale":"Each external system has its own data model and protocol. ACLs prevent model pollution from leaking SAP XML structures, DHL status codes, or analytics schemas into the core domain. Each adapter owns its translation independently."},{"pattern":"Transactional Outbox","rationale":"The SAP adapter uses an outbox pattern to reliably capture OrderConfirmed events in a local table before the SFTP scheduler flushes them as a batch. This prevents lost messages if SFTP is temporarily unavailable and supports exactly-once delivery semantics with idempotent file naming."},{"pattern":"Async Webhook Acknowledgment (Thin Gateway)","rationale":"The 500ms SLA on DHL webhook responses demands that we decouple HTTP acknowledgment from business processing. The gateway validates and ACKs immediately, then enqueues for async domain processing. This keeps p99 latency well under 500ms regardless of domain processing time."},{"pattern":"Event-Driven Architecture (Internal Event Bus)","rationale":"All integration adapters subscribe to domain events rather than being called directly by domain services. This means adding a new external system (e.g., a second carrier) requires only a new adapter subscription — zero changes to domain logic."},{"pattern":"Published Language (Analytics Schema)","rationale":"The analytics adapter owns a stable, versioned schema (via schema registry) that the Analytics Platform consumes. This decouples the internal domain model evolution from the external analytics contract."},{"pattern":"Hexagonal Architecture (Ports and Adapters)","rationale":"Domain services define ports (event publishing, command interfaces). Each external integration is an adapter plugged into these ports. The domain core has zero knowledge of SFTP, REST, webhooks, or Kafka."}]},"domain_model":{"bounded_contexts":[{"name":"Order Management","classification":"core","aggregates":["Order"]},{"name":"Shipment Management","classification":"core","aggregates":["Shipment"]},{"name":"ERP Integration","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["SapExportRecord"]},{"name":"Carrier Integration","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["CarrierBooking"]},{"name":"Analytics Projection","classification":"generic","aggregates":[]}],"context_map":[{"upstream":"Order Management","downstream":"Shipment Management","relationship":"Customer-Supplier — Order Management publishes OrderConfirmed; Shipment Management consumes it to initiate shipment creation. Order Management owns the event schema."},{"upstream":"Order Management","downstream":"ERP Integration","relationship":"Customer-Supplier — ERP Integration consumes OrderConfirmed and translates to SAP XML. ACL prevents SAP model from influencing Order Management."},{"upstream":"Shipment Management","downstream":"Carrier Integration","relationship":"Customer-Supplier — Carrier Integration consumes ShipmentCreated to book with DHL and feeds back delivery status via commands. ACL isolates DHL data model."},{"upstream":"Carrier Integration","downstream":"Shipment Management","relationship":"Conformist (inbound) — When DHL sends webhooks, the Carrier Integration adapter conforms to DHL's callback schema externally but translates to domain commands internally."},{"upstream":"Order Management","downstream":"Analytics Projection","relationship":"Open Host Service / Published Language — Analytics consumes all domain events through a stable published schema."},{"upstream":"Shipment Management","downstream":"Analytics Projection","relationship":"Open Host Service / Published Language — Same pattern; analytics subscribes to all shipment events."}]},"trade_offs":[{"decision":"Async webhook processing with immediate ACK instead of synchronous domain processing","gains":"Guaranteed sub-500ms response to DHL. Resilient to domain processing slowdowns. Gateway can be scaled independently.","sacrifices":"Eventual consistency — DHL gets 200 ACK before we've actually processed the status update. If domain processing fails, we must handle it via retry/dead-letter rather than returning an error to DHL.","alternatives_considered":["Synchronous processing in webhook handler — rejected because domain logic latency is unpredictable and risks breaching 500ms SLA","Circuit breaker on sync path — partial mitigation but still couples ACK to processing speed"]},{"decision":"Transactional outbox for SAP SFTP batching instead of direct event-to-SFTP","gains":"Reliable delivery even when SFTP is down. Natural batching aligns with SAP's 15-minute polling. Supports replay and audit.","sacrifices":"Added complexity of outbox table and scheduler. Slight increase in latency (up to 15 minutes by design, which matches the requirement).","alternatives_considered":["Direct SFTP write per event — rejected because high frequency individual files would overwhelm SAP and lack atomicity","Polling-based export from database — rejected because it couples SAP integration to internal database schema"]},{"decision":"Single internal event bus rather than point-to-point integration","gains":"All adapters are decoupled from each other and from domain services. Adding a new external system is additive, not modificative. Single place to monitor event flow.","sacrifices":"Operational overhead of running a message broker. All integrations have at-least-once semantics requiring idempotency in consumers.","alternatives_considered":["Direct service-to-adapter calls — rejected because it creates tight coupling and makes adding new integrations expensive","Shared database for integration state — rejected because it violates bounded context boundaries"]},{"decision":"Separate ACL per external system rather than a shared integration layer","gains":"Each adapter evolves independently. SAP XML schema changes don't affect DHL integration. Clear ownership boundaries. Easier to test in isolation.","sacrifices":"Some structural duplication across adapters (retry logic, error handling). Slightly more code than a unified gateway.","alternatives_considered":["Unified integration gateway — rejected because it creates a God object that couples all external system concerns together","Shared integration library — acceptable for cross-cutting concerns like retry/circuit-breaker, but each adapter must own its own translation logic"]}],"flexibility_points":[{"area":"Adding new carriers","evolution_path":"Create a new Carrier Integration Adapter that subscribes to ShipmentCreated. Implement the carrier-specific ACL. No changes to Shipment Domain Service or other adapters. The CarrierBooking aggregate can be extended with a carrier type discriminator."},{"area":"Replacing the internal event bus implementation","evolution_path":"Domain services publish events through a port interface. Swap the underlying broker (e.g., from RabbitMQ to Kafka, or in-process for testing) without touching domain code."},{"area":"Adding new domain events","evolution_path":"Define a new event, publish it from the appropriate domain service. Each adapter independently decides whether to subscribe. Analytics adapter likely subscribes to all new events; other adapters only if relevant."},{"area":"SAP batch frequency changes","evolution_path":"The SFTP Scheduler's cron expression is a configuration value. Changing from 15 minutes to 5 minutes requires zero code changes."},{"area":"Analytics schema evolution","evolution_path":"Schema registry supports backward-compatible evolution. New fields are added as optional. Breaking changes use a new topic version with a migration period."}],"rigidity_points":[{"area":"Domain event schema","reason":"All adapters depend on the internal event schema. Changes to event structure (renaming fields, removing fields) require coordinated updates across all consumers. This is intentional — the domain model should be stable and changes should be deliberate."},{"area":"Webhook response SLA (500ms)","reason":"This is a hard constraint from DHL. The architecture is specifically designed around it. Changing to synchronous processing would require a fundamental redesign of the webhook handling path."},{"area":"SFTP protocol for SAP","reason":"SAP's integration contract is SFTP + XML. This is externally imposed and unlikely to change without a major SAP migration. The ACL isolates the impact, but the adapter itself is locked to this protocol."}],"risks":[{"risk":"Webhook payload processing failure after ACK — DHL believes delivery was acknowledged but domain rejects it","severity":"high","mitigation":"Dead-letter queue for failed webhook payloads. Alerting on DLQ depth. Manual or automated retry mechanism. Reconciliation job that compares DHL shipment states with internal states periodically."},{"risk":"SAP SFTP outbox grows unboundedly if SFTP is down for extended period","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Monitoring on outbox table size. Circuit breaker that alerts operations if SFTP failures exceed threshold. Configurable retention policy on outbox records."},{"risk":"Event bus becomes single point of failure — all integrations stop if the broker is down","severity":"critical","mitigation":"Run the event bus in a clustered, highly available configuration. Implement local buffering in producers so events are not lost during brief outages. Health checks and automated failover."},{"risk":"Kafka producer backpressure from Analytics Platform causes internal event bus consumer lag","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Analytics adapter has its own consumer group with independent backpressure handling. Buffer between internal bus consumption and Kafka production. Circuit breaker on Kafka producer that drops to buffered mode rather than blocking."},{"risk":"DHL API rate limiting or downtime blocks shipment booking","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Exponential backoff with jitter on outbound DHL calls. Circuit breaker pattern. Queue of pending bookings that drain when DHL recovers. Alert on booking queue depth."},{"risk":"Event ordering — out-of-order delivery causes DeliveryCompleted before ShipmentCreated in analytics","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Partition events by aggregate ID (orderId/shipmentId) to maintain per-entity ordering. Analytics adapter uses event timestamps for sequencing. Idempotent consumers handle duplicates."}],"implementation_guidance":{"recommended_approach":"Implement in three phases. Phase 1: Stand up the internal event bus and domain services with all six events flowing. Phase 2: Build the three ACL adapters in parallel (SAP outbox + SFTP scheduler, DHL outbound + webhook gateway, Analytics Kafka producer) since they are independent of each other. Phase 3: End-to-end integration testing with contract tests per adapter and chaos testing on the event bus. Start with the DHL webhook path since it has the tightest SLA constraint — prove the 500ms guarantee early.","files_to_create_or_modify":["src/domain/order/OrderService.ts — Order aggregate and event publishing","src/domain/shipment/ShipmentService.ts — Shipment aggregate and event publishing","src/domain/events/DomainEvents.ts — Event type definitions for all six events","src/infrastructure/eventbus/EventBusPort.ts — Port interface for event publishing/subscribing","src/infrastructure/eventbus/EventBusAdapter.ts — Concrete broker implementation","src/integration/sap/SapAcl.ts — SAP XML translation and outbox writes","src/integration/sap/SapSftpScheduler.ts — Cron-triggered SFTP batch upload","src/integration/sap/schema/OrderConfirmedXml.ts — SAP XML schema mapping","src/integration/dhl/DhlAcl.ts — DHL REST client and status translation","src/integration/dhl/DhlWebhookGateway.ts — Thin HTTP endpoint, validate + ACK + enqueue","src/integration/dhl/DhlStatusMapper.ts — Maps DHL status codes to domain events","src/integration/analytics/AnalyticsStreamAdapter.ts — Kafka producer with schema registry","src/integration/analytics/schema/ — Avro/JSON schema definitions for analytics events","tests/integration/sap/ — Contract tests for SAP XML generation and SFTP upload","tests/integration/dhl/ — Contract tests for DHL API calls and webhook handling","tests/integration/analytics/ — Contract tests for Kafka event schema compliance"],"patterns_to_follow":"Hexagonal Architecture with explicit port interfaces. Each integration adapter implements the port contract. Domain services depend only on port interfaces, never on adapter implementations. Use dependency injection to wire adapters at composition root. Transactional outbox uses the same database transaction as domain state changes to guarantee consistency. All consumers must be idempotent — use event IDs for deduplication.","pitfalls_to_avoid":["Do NOT process DHL webhooks synchronously — the 500ms SLA will be violated under load","Do NOT let SAP XML types appear in domain code — keep the ACL boundary strict","Do NOT share a single Kafka topic for internal events and analytics events — these are different bounded contexts with different schemas and retention policies","Do NOT use a shared integration adapter for multiple external systems — each ACL must be independent even if they look similar today","Do NOT skip the outbox pattern for SAP — writing directly to SFTP from an event handler creates data loss risk on SFTP failures","Do NOT assume event ordering across different aggregate types — only guarantee ordering within a single aggregate ID partition"]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":88,"high_confidence_areas":["Webhook async ACK pattern is well-established for sub-second SLA requirements","ACL-per-external-system is the correct DDD integration pattern here","Transactional outbox for SFTP batching fits the 15-minute schedule naturally","Event-driven internal architecture is appropriate given three diverse downstream consumers","The six domain events map cleanly to order and shipment aggregate lifecycles"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact internal event bus technology choice (Kafka vs RabbitMQ vs cloud-native) depends on existing infrastructure which is unspecified","Whether CarrierBooking needs to be a full aggregate or a simpler projection depends on how much carrier-specific state the platform needs to track","Schema registry choice for analytics depends on whether the analytics platform has existing preferences"],"assumptions":["The logistics platform owns its own database and can implement the outbox pattern","DHL webhook callbacks include sufficient authentication (signature/HMAC) for validation","SAP SFTP endpoint is managed by the ERP team and the XML schema is provided","The analytics platform's Kafka cluster is externally managed — the logistics platform is a producer only","Event volume is moderate (thousands/day, not millions) — if volume is very high, additional partitioning and scaling considerations apply"]}}

Let me design the integration architecture for this logistics platform with three external systems.

Classification: `none` — sufficient constraints exist. I have clear NFRs (500ms webhook response, 15-min SFTP batch, real-time Kafka stream), well-defined integration boundaries, and specific event types.

Resilient to domain processing slowdowns. Gateway can be scaled independently.","sacrifices":"Eventual consistency — DHL gets 200 ACK before we've actually processed the status update. Natural batching aligns with SAP's 15-minute polling. Supports replay and audit.","sacrifices":"Added complexity of outbox table and scheduler. Slight increase in latency (up to 15 minutes by design, which matches the requirement).","alternatives_considered":["Direct SFTP write per event — rejected because high frequency individual files would overwhelm SAP and lack atomicity","Polling-based export from database — rejected because it couples SAP integration to internal database schema"]},{"decision":"Single internal event bus rather than point-to-point integration","gains":"All adapters are decoupled from each other and from domain services. Adding a new external system is additive, not modificative. Single place to monitor event flow.","sacrifices":"Operational overhead of running a message broker. All integrations have at-least-once semantics requiring idempotency in consumers.","alternatives_considered":["Direct service-to-adapter calls — rejected because it creates tight coupling and makes adding new integrations expensive","Shared database for integration state — rejected because it violates bounded context boundaries"]},{"decision":"Separate ACL per external system rather than a shared integration layer","gains":"Each adapter evolves independently. SAP XML schema changes don't affect DHL integration. Clear ownership boundaries. Easier to test in isolation.","sacrifices":"Some structural duplication across adapters (retry logic, error handling). Slightly more code than a unified gateway.","alternatives_considered":["Unified integration gateway — rejected because it creates a God object that couples all external system concerns together","Shared integration library — acceptable for cross-cutting concerns like retry/circuit-breaker, but each adapter must own its own translation logic"]}],"flexibility_points":[{"area":"Adding new carriers","evolution_path":"Create a new Carrier Integration Adapter that subscribes to ShipmentCreated. Implement the carrier-specific ACL. No changes to Shipment Domain Service or other adapters. The CarrierBooking aggregate can be extended with a carrier type discriminator."},{"area":"Replacing the internal event bus implementation","evolution_path":"Domain services publish events through a port interface. Swap the underlying broker (e.g., from RabbitMQ to Kafka, or in-process for testing) without touching domain code."},{"area":"Adding new domain events","evolution_path":"Define a new event, publish it from the appropriate domain service. Each adapter independently decides whether to subscribe. Analytics adapter likely subscribes to all new events; other adapters only if relevant."},{"area":"SAP batch frequency changes","evolution_path":"The SFTP Scheduler's cron expression is a configuration value. Changing from 15 minutes to 5 minutes requires zero code changes."},{"area":"Analytics schema evolution","evolution_path":"Schema registry supports backward-compatible evolution. New fields are added as optional. Breaking changes use a new topic version with a migration period."}],"rigidity_points":[{"area":"Domain event schema","reason":"All adapters depend on the internal event schema. Changes to event structure (renaming fields, removing fields) require coordinated updates across all consumers. This is intentional — the domain model should be stable and changes should be deliberate."},{"area":"Webhook response SLA (500ms)","reason":"This is a hard constraint from DHL. The architecture is specifically designed around it. Changing to synchronous processing would require a fundamental redesign of the webhook handling path."},{"area":"SFTP protocol for SAP","reason":"SAP's integration contract is SFTP + XML. This is externally imposed and unlikely to change without a major SAP migration. The ACL isolates the impact, but the adapter itself is locked to this protocol."}],"risks":[{"risk":"Webhook payload processing failure after ACK — DHL believes delivery was acknowledged but domain rejects it","severity":"high","mitigation":"Dead-letter queue for failed webhook payloads. Alerting on DLQ depth. Manual or automated retry mechanism. Reconciliation job that compares DHL shipment states with internal states periodically."},{"risk":"SAP SFTP outbox grows unboundedly if SFTP is down for extended period","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Monitoring on outbox table size. Circuit breaker that alerts operations if SFTP failures exceed threshold. Configurable retention policy on outbox records."},{"risk":"Event bus becomes single point of failure — all integrations stop if the broker is down","severity":"critical","mitigation":"Run the event bus in a clustered, highly available configuration. Implement local buffering in producers so events are not lost during brief outages. Health checks and automated failover."},{"risk":"Kafka producer backpressure from Analytics Platform causes internal event bus consumer lag","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Analytics adapter has its own consumer group with independent backpressure handling. Buffer between internal bus consumption and Kafka production. Circuit breaker on Kafka producer that drops to buffered mode rather than blocking."},{"risk":"DHL API rate limiting or downtime blocks shipment booking","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Exponential backoff with jitter on outbound DHL calls. Circuit breaker pattern. Queue of pending bookings that drain when DHL recovers. Alert on booking queue depth."},{"risk":"Event ordering — out-of-order delivery causes DeliveryCompleted before ShipmentCreated in analytics","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Partition events by aggregate ID (orderId/shipmentId) to maintain per-entity ordering. Analytics adapter uses event timestamps for sequencing. Idempotent consumers handle duplicates."}],"implementation_guidance":{"recommended_approach":"Implement in three phases. Phase 1: Stand up the internal event bus and domain services with all six events flowing. Phase 2: Build the three ACL adapters in parallel (SAP outbox + SFTP scheduler, DHL outbound + webhook gateway, Analytics Kafka producer) since they are independent of each other. Phase 3: End-to-end integration testing with contract tests per adapter and chaos testing on the event bus. Start with the DHL webhook path since it has the tightest SLA constraint — prove the 500ms guarantee early.","files_to_create_or_modify":["src/domain/order/OrderService.ts — Order aggregate and event publishing","src/domain/shipment/ShipmentService.ts — Shipment aggregate and event publishing","src/domain/events/DomainEvents.ts — Event type definitions for all six events","src/infrastructure/eventbus/EventBusPort.ts — Port interface for event publishing/subscribing","src/infrastructure/eventbus/EventBusAdapter.ts — Concrete broker implementation","src/integration/sap/SapAcl.ts — SAP XML translation and outbox writes","src/integration/sap/SapSftpScheduler.ts — Cron-triggered SFTP batch upload","src/integration/sap/schema/OrderConfirmedXml.ts — SAP XML schema mapping","src/integration/dhl/DhlAcl.ts — DHL REST client and status translation","src/integration/dhl/DhlWebhookGateway.ts — Thin HTTP endpoint, validate + ACK + enqueue","src/integration/dhl/DhlStatusMapper.ts — Maps DHL status codes to domain events","src/integration/analytics/AnalyticsStreamAdapter.ts — Kafka producer with schema registry","src/integration/analytics/schema/ — Avro/JSON schema definitions for analytics events","tests/integration/sap/ — Contract tests for SAP XML generation and SFTP upload","tests/integration/dhl/ — Contract tests for DHL API calls and webhook handling","tests/integration/analytics/ — Contract tests for Kafka event schema compliance"],"patterns_to_follow":"Hexagonal Architecture with explicit port interfaces. Each integration adapter implements the port contract. Domain services depend only on port interfaces, never on adapter implementations. Use dependency injection to wire adapters at composition root. Transactional outbox uses the same database transaction as domain state changes to guarantee consistency. All consumers must be idempotent — use event IDs for deduplication.","pitfalls_to_avoid":["Do NOT process DHL webhooks synchronously — the 500ms SLA will be violated under load","Do NOT let SAP XML types appear in domain code — keep the ACL boundary strict","Do NOT share a single Kafka topic for internal events and analytics events — these are different bounded contexts with different schemas and retention policies","Do NOT use a shared integration adapter for multiple external systems — each ACL must be independent even if they look similar today","Do NOT skip the outbox pattern for SAP — writing directly to SFTP from an event handler creates data loss risk on SFTP failures","Do NOT assume event ordering across different aggregate types — only guarantee ordering within a single aggregate ID partition"]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":88,"high_confidence_areas":["Webhook async ACK pattern is well-established for sub-second SLA requirements","ACL-per-external-system is the correct DDD integration pattern here","Transactional outbox for SFTP batching fits the 15-minute schedule naturally","Event-driven internal architecture is appropriate given three diverse downstream consumers","The six domain events map cleanly to order and shipment aggregate lifecycles"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact internal event bus technology choice (Kafka vs RabbitMQ vs cloud-native) depends on existing infrastructure which is unspecified","Whether CarrierBooking needs to be a full aggregate or a simpler projection depends on how much carrier-specific state the platform needs to track","Schema registry choice for analytics depends on whether the analytics platform has existing preferences"],"assumptions":["The logistics platform owns its own database and can implement the outbox pattern","DHL webhook callbacks include sufficient authentication (signature/HMAC) for validation","SAP SFTP endpoint is managed by the ERP team and the XML schema is provided","The analytics platform's Kafka cluster is externally managed — the logistics platform is a producer only","Event volume is moderate (thousands/day, not millions) — if volume is very high, additional partitioning and scaling considerations apply"]}}

trace.result: {"executive_summary":"Integration architecture for a logistics platform connecting three external systems (SAP ERP via SFTP/XML, DHL Carrier API via REST/webhooks, Analytics Platform via Kafka). The design uses an Anti-Corruption Layer per external system, a central domain event bus for internal decoupling, and distinct integration adapters that translate between the platform's ubiquitous language and each external system's protocol and data model. The architecture ensures that no external system's model leaks into the core domain, each integration point can evolve independently, and the 500ms webhook SLA is met through async-first processing.","architecture":{"components":[{"name":"Order Domain Service","responsibility":"Owns the lifecycle of orders: placement, confirmation, and all associated business rules and invariants.","boundaries":"Publishes OrderPlaced, OrderConfirmed domain events. Never directly communicates with external systems. Only speaks the internal ubiquitous language."},{"name":"Shipment Domain Service","responsibility":"Owns shipment lifecycle: creation, delivery attempts, completion, failure. Enforces shipment invariants (e.g., cannot complete a failed shipment).","boundaries":"Publishes ShipmentCreated, DeliveryAttempted, DeliveryCompleted, DeliveryFailed. Consumes OrderConfirmed to trigger shipment creation workflows."},{"name":"Internal Event Bus","responsibility":"Mediates all domain events between internal services. Provides guaranteed at-least-once delivery, ordering per aggregate, and replay capability.","boundaries":"Internal only. No external system connects directly. Implemented as a durable message broker (e.g., internal Kafka topics or similar). All domain events flow through here before reaching any integration adapter."},{"name":"SAP ERP Integration Adapter (ACL)","responsibility":"Anti-Corruption Layer for System A. Subscribes to OrderConfirmed events, transforms them into SAP-specific XML format, batches them, and delivers via SFTP on the 15-minute schedule. Also handles SFTP connection management, retry, and file naming conventions.","boundaries":"Owns the SAP XML schema mapping. Encapsulates SFTP protocol details. No SAP concepts leak upstream. Maintains its own outbox table for reliable batching."},{"name":"DHL Carrier Integration Adapter (ACL)","responsibility":"Anti-Corruption Layer for System B. Two sub-components: (1) Outbound — subscribes to ShipmentCreated events and calls DHL REST API to book shipments. (2) Inbound — exposes a webhook endpoint that receives DHL status callbacks, validates/authenticates them, acknowledges within 500ms, and translates DHL status models into internal domain events (DeliveryAttempted, DeliveryCompleted, DeliveryFailed).","boundaries":"Owns the DHL data model translation. Webhook handler is deliberately thin — acknowledges immediately and enqueues for async domain processing. No DHL-specific types escape this adapter."},{"name":"Analytics Event Stream Adapter","responsibility":"Subscribes to ALL domain events on the internal event bus and projects them onto the external Kafka topic(s) in the Analytics Platform's expected schema. Handles schema evolution and backward compatibility.","boundaries":"Read-only consumer of internal events. Owns the analytics event schema (Published Language). Does not feed data back into the domain."},{"name":"Webhook Gateway","responsibility":"Thin HTTP layer that receives inbound webhooks from DHL (and potentially other carriers in the future). Validates signatures, performs rate limiting, returns 200 ACK within the 500ms SLA, and enqueues the raw payload for async processing by the DHL Integration Adapter.","boundaries":"Protocol-level only. No business logic. Stateless. Horizontally scalable."},{"name":"SFTP Scheduler","responsibility":"Cron-triggered component that flushes the SAP outbox every 15 minutes. Assembles queued OrderConfirmed XML documents into a single batch file, uploads via SFTP, and marks records as sent.","boundaries":"Operates on the SAP adapter's outbox. Handles SFTP connection pooling, retries with exponential backoff, and duplicate suppression via idempotency keys in filenames."}],"interfaces":[{"from":"Order Domain Service","to":"Internal Event Bus","contract":"Publishes OrderPlaced{orderId, timestamp, lineItems, customer} and OrderConfirmed{orderId, timestamp, confirmedBy}","coupling_type":"event (async, fire-and-forget)"},{"from":"Shipment Domain Service","to":"Internal Event Bus","contract":"Publishes ShipmentCreated{shipmentId, orderId, carrier, trackingNumber}, DeliveryAttempted{shipmentId, attemptNumber, timestamp}, DeliveryCompleted{shipmentId, timestamp, signature}, DeliveryFailed{shipmentId, reason, timestamp}","coupling_type":"event (async, fire-and-forget)"},{"from":"Internal Event Bus","to":"SAP ERP Integration Adapter","contract":"Delivers OrderConfirmed events. Adapter transforms to SAP XML and writes to outbox.","coupling_type":"event subscription (async, at-least-once)"},{"from":"SAP ERP Integration Adapter","to":"SAP ERP (System A)","contract":"SFTP upload of XML batch file conforming to SAP IDOC schema. Filename contains timestamp and sequence number for idempotency.","coupling_type":"file transfer (batch, scheduled)"},{"from":"Internal Event Bus","to":"DHL Carrier Integration Adapter","contract":"Delivers ShipmentCreated events. Adapter calls DHL booking API.","coupling_type":"event subscription (async, at-least-once)"},{"from":"DHL Carrier Integration Adapter","to":"DHL API (System B)","contract":"REST POST to DHL shipment booking endpoint. Expects 201 with tracking number. Retries with exponential backoff on 5xx.","coupling_type":"synchronous REST (outbound call)"},{"from":"DHL API (System B)","to":"Webhook Gateway","contract":"HTTP POST callback with DHL shipment status payload. Gateway returns 200 within 500ms.","coupling_type":"webhook (inbound, async processing after ACK)"},{"from":"Webhook Gateway","to":"DHL Carrier Integration Adapter","contract":"Enqueues raw DHL webhook payload onto internal processing queue for translation into domain events.","coupling_type":"queue (async, at-least-once)"},{"from":"DHL Carrier Integration Adapter","to":"Shipment Domain Service","contract":"Calls command methods (RecordDeliveryAttempt, CompleteDelivery, FailDelivery) after translating DHL status.","coupling_type":"synchronous command invocation"},{"from":"Internal Event Bus","to":"Analytics Event Stream Adapter","contract":"All six domain event types. Adapter transforms to analytics schema and produces to Kafka.","coupling_type":"event subscription (async, at-least-once)"},{"from":"Analytics Event Stream Adapter","to":"Analytics Platform (System C)","contract":"Kafka producer writing to designated topic(s) in Avro/JSON schema with schema registry for evolution.","coupling_type":"event stream (async, real-time)"}],"data_flow":"1. OrderPlaced → Internal Event Bus → Analytics Adapter → Kafka (System C). 2. OrderConfirmed → Internal Event Bus → (a) SAP Adapter → outbox → SFTP Scheduler → XML batch → SAP (System A), (b) Shipment Domain Service triggers ShipmentCreated, (c) Analytics Adapter → Kafka. 3. ShipmentCreated → Internal Event Bus → (a) DHL Adapter → REST call → DHL API (System B), (b) Analytics Adapter → Kafka. 4. DHL webhook → Webhook Gateway → ACK 200 → queue → DHL Adapter → translates to DeliveryAttempted/Completed/Failed → Shipment Domain Service → domain event → Internal Event Bus → Analytics Adapter → Kafka.","patterns_used":[{"pattern":"Anti-Corruption Layer (per external system)","rationale":"Each external system has its own data model and protocol. ACLs prevent model pollution from leaking SAP XML structures, DHL status codes, or analytics schemas into the core domain. Each adapter owns its translation independently."},{"pattern":"Transactional Outbox","rationale":"The SAP adapter uses an outbox pattern to reliably capture OrderConfirmed events in a local table before the SFTP scheduler flushes them as a batch. This prevents lost messages if SFTP is temporarily unavailable and supports exactly-once delivery semantics with idempotent file naming."},{"pattern":"Async Webhook Acknowledgment (Thin Gateway)","rationale":"The 500ms SLA on DHL webhook responses demands that we decouple HTTP acknowledgment from business processing. The gateway validates and ACKs immediately, then enqueues for async domain processing. This keeps p99 latency well under 500ms regardless of domain processing time."},{"pattern":"Event-Driven Architecture (Internal Event Bus)","rationale":"All integration adapters subscribe to domain events rather than being called directly by domain services. This means adding a new external system (e.g., a second carrier) requires only a new adapter subscription — zero changes to domain logic."},{"pattern":"Published Language (Analytics Schema)","rationale":"The analytics adapter owns a stable, versioned schema (via schema registry) that the Analytics Platform consumes. This decouples the internal domain model evolution from the external analytics contract."},{"pattern":"Hexagonal Architecture (Ports and Adapters)","rationale":"Domain services define ports (event publishing, command interfaces). Each external integration is an adapter plugged into these ports. The domain core has zero knowledge of SFTP, REST, webhooks, or Kafka."}]},"domain_model":{"bounded_contexts":[{"name":"Order Management","classification":"core","aggregates":["Order"]},{"name":"Shipment Management","classification":"core","aggregates":["Shipment"]},{"name":"ERP Integration","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["SapExportRecord"]},{"name":"Carrier Integration","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["CarrierBooking"]},{"name":"Analytics Projection","classification":"generic","aggregates":[]}],"context_map":[{"upstream":"Order Management","downstream":"Shipment Management","relationship":"Customer-Supplier — Order Management publishes OrderConfirmed; Shipment Management consumes it to initiate shipment creation. Order Management owns the event schema."},{"upstream":"Order Management","downstream":"ERP Integration","relationship":"Customer-Supplier — ERP Integration consumes OrderConfirmed and translates to SAP XML. ACL prevents SAP model from influencing Order Management."},{"upstream":"Shipment Management","downstream":"Carrier Integration","relationship":"Customer-Supplier — Carrier Integration consumes ShipmentCreated to book with DHL and feeds back delivery status via commands. ACL isolates DHL data model."},{"upstream":"Carrier Integration","downstream":"Shipment Management","relationship":"Conformist (inbound) — When DHL sends webhooks, the Carrier Integration adapter conforms to DHL's callback schema externally but translates to domain commands internally."},{"upstream":"Order Management","downstream":"Analytics Projection","relationship":"Open Host Service / Published Language — Analytics consumes all domain events through a stable published schema."},{"upstream":"Shipment Management","downstream":"Analytics Projection","relationship":"Open Host Service / Published Language — Same pattern; analytics subscribes to all shipment events."}]},"trade_offs":[{"decision":"Async webhook processing with immediate ACK instead of synchronous domain processing","gains":"Guaranteed sub-500ms response to DHL. Resilient to domain processing slowdowns. Gateway can be scaled independently.","sacrifices":"Eventual consistency — DHL gets 200 ACK before we've actually processed the status update. If domain processing fails, we must handle it via retry/dead-letter rather than returning an error to DHL.","alternatives_considered":["Synchronous processing in webhook handler — rejected because domain logic latency is unpredictable and risks breaching 500ms SLA","Circuit breaker on sync path — partial mitigation but still couples ACK to processing speed"]},{"decision":"Transactional outbox for SAP SFTP batching instead of direct event-to-SFTP","gains":"Reliable delivery even when SFTP is down. Natural batching aligns with SAP's 15-minute polling. Supports replay and audit.","sacrifices":"Added complexity of outbox table and scheduler. Slight increase in latency (up to 15 minutes by design, which matches the requirement).","alternatives_considered":["Direct SFTP write per event — rejected because high frequency individual files would overwhelm SAP and lack atomicity","Polling-based export from database — rejected because it couples SAP integration to internal database schema"]},{"decision":"Single internal event bus rather than point-to-point integration","gains":"All adapters are decoupled from each other and from domain services. Adding a new external system is additive, not modificative. Single place to monitor event flow.","sacrifices":"Operational overhead of running a message broker. All integrations have at-least-once semantics requiring idempotency in consumers.","alternatives_considered":["Direct service-to-adapter calls — rejected because it creates tight coupling and makes adding new integrations expensive","Shared database for integration state — rejected because it violates bounded context boundaries"]},{"decision":"Separate ACL per external system rather than a shared integration layer","gains":"Each adapter evolves independently. SAP XML schema changes don't affect DHL integration. Clear ownership boundaries. Easier to test in isolation.","sacrifices":"Some structural duplication across adapters (retry logic, error handling). Slightly more code than a unified gateway.","alternatives_considered":["Unified integration gateway — rejected because it creates a God object that couples all external system concerns together","Shared integration library — acceptable for cross-cutting concerns like retry/circuit-breaker, but each adapter must own its own translation logic"]}],"flexibility_points":[{"area":"Adding new carriers","evolution_path":"Create a new Carrier Integration Adapter that subscribes to ShipmentCreated. Implement the carrier-specific ACL. No changes to Shipment Domain Service or other adapters. The CarrierBooking aggregate can be extended with a carrier type discriminator."},{"area":"Replacing the internal event bus implementation","evolution_path":"Domain services publish events through a port interface. Swap the underlying broker (e.g., from RabbitMQ to Kafka, or in-process for testing) without touching domain code."},{"area":"Adding new domain events","evolution_path":"Define a new event, publish it from the appropriate domain service. Each adapter independently decides whether to subscribe. Analytics adapter likely subscribes to all new events; other adapters only if relevant."},{"area":"SAP batch frequency changes","evolution_path":"The SFTP Scheduler's cron expression is a configuration value. Changing from 15 minutes to 5 minutes requires zero code changes."},{"area":"Analytics schema evolution","evolution_path":"Schema registry supports backward-compatible evolution. New fields are added as optional. Breaking changes use a new topic version with a migration period."}],"rigidity_points":[{"area":"Domain event schema","reason":"All adapters depend on the internal event schema. Changes to event structure (renaming fields, removing fields) require coordinated updates across all consumers. This is intentional — the domain model should be stable and changes should be deliberate."},{"area":"Webhook response SLA (500ms)","reason":"This is a hard constraint from DHL. The architecture is specifically designed around it. Changing to synchronous processing would require a fundamental redesign of the webhook handling path."},{"area":"SFTP protocol for SAP","reason":"SAP's integration contract is SFTP + XML. This is externally imposed and unlikely to change without a major SAP migration. The ACL isolates the impact, but the adapter itself is locked to this protocol."}],"risks":[{"risk":"Webhook payload processing failure after ACK — DHL believes delivery was acknowledged but domain rejects it","severity":"high","mitigation":"Dead-letter queue for failed webhook payloads. Alerting on DLQ depth. Manual or automated retry mechanism. Reconciliation job that compares DHL shipment states with internal states periodically."},{"risk":"SAP SFTP outbox grows unboundedly if SFTP is down for extended period","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Monitoring on outbox table size. Circuit breaker that alerts operations if SFTP failures exceed threshold. Configurable retention policy on outbox records."},{"risk":"Event bus becomes single point of failure — all integrations stop if the broker is down","severity":"critical","mitigation":"Run the event bus in a clustered, highly available configuration. Implement local buffering in producers so events are not lost during brief outages. Health checks and automated failover."},{"risk":"Kafka producer backpressure from Analytics Platform causes internal event bus consumer lag","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Analytics adapter has its own consumer group with independent backpressure handling. Buffer between internal bus consumption and Kafka production. Circuit breaker on Kafka producer that drops to buffered mode rather than blocking."},{"risk":"DHL API rate limiting or downtime blocks shipment booking","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Exponential backoff with jitter on outbound DHL calls. Circuit breaker pattern. Queue of pending bookings that drain when DHL recovers. Alert on booking queue depth."},{"risk":"Event ordering — out-of-order delivery causes DeliveryCompleted before ShipmentCreated in analytics","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Partition events by aggregate ID (orderId/shipmentId) to maintain per-entity ordering. Analytics adapter uses event timestamps for sequencing. Idempotent consumers handle duplicates."}],"implementation_guidance":{"recommended_approach":"Implement in three phases. Phase 1: Stand up the internal event bus and domain services with all six events flowing. Phase 2: Build the three ACL adapters in parallel (SAP outbox + SFTP scheduler, DHL outbound + webhook gateway, Analytics Kafka producer) since they are independent of each other. Phase 3: End-to-end integration testing with contract tests per adapter and chaos testing on the event bus. Start with the DHL webhook path since it has the tightest SLA constraint — prove the 500ms guarantee early.","files_to_create_or_modify":["src/domain/order/OrderService.ts — Order aggregate and event publishing","src/domain/shipment/ShipmentService.ts — Shipment aggregate and event publishing","src/domain/events/DomainEvents.ts — Event type definitions for all six events","src/infrastructure/eventbus/EventBusPort.ts — Port interface for event publishing/subscribing","src/infrastructure/eventbus/EventBusAdapter.ts — Concrete broker implementation","src/integration/sap/SapAcl.ts — SAP XML translation and outbox writes","src/integration/sap/SapSftpScheduler.ts — Cron-triggered SFTP batch upload","src/integration/sap/schema/OrderConfirmedXml.ts — SAP XML schema mapping","src/integration/dhl/DhlAcl.ts — DHL REST client and status translation","src/integration/dhl/DhlWebhookGateway.ts — Thin HTTP endpoint, validate + ACK + enqueue","src/integration/dhl/DhlStatusMapper.ts — Maps DHL status codes to domain events","src/integration/analytics/AnalyticsStreamAdapter.ts — Kafka producer with schema registry","src/integration/analytics/schema/ — Avro/JSON schema definitions for analytics events","tests/integration/sap/ — Contract tests for SAP XML generation and SFTP upload","tests/integration/dhl/ — Contract tests for DHL API calls and webhook handling","tests/integration/analytics/ — Contract tests for Kafka event schema compliance"],"patterns_to_follow":"Hexagonal Architecture with explicit port interfaces.
